Uttarakhand Weather: Heatwave intensifies in plains, Dehradun records 33°C, Rain prediction after April 16
On April 14, Uttarakhand faces dry weather and rising heat. Dehradun recorded a maximum temperature of 33°C, troubling residents in the plains. While the heatwave worsens conditions, mountain regions may see changes from April 16 with rain and thundershowers predicted in higher districts.
Dehradun: The weather in Uttarakhand has turned harsh, with the sun blazing across the plains. On Tuesday, April 14, the Meteorological Center reported dry conditions throughout the state.
In Dehradun, the maximum temperature has already touched 33 degrees Celsius, making daily life difficult for people.
Heatwave grips the plains
For the past four to five days, temperatures have been rising steadily in Dehradun and other plain districts. Bright sunshine since morning has forced people indoors, as venturing out during the day has become uncomfortable. The dry weather is expected to continue today and tomorrow, which could push temperatures even higher.
Gangotri trekking track update
Meanwhile, in Uttarkashi, a team inspecting the trekking track in Gangotri National Park had to return due to heavy snow. Officials said that if no fresh snowfall occurs in the higher elevations this week, the trek may reopen for mountaineers. However, ordinary travelers will have to wait longer before visiting Gomukh.
Weather change expected in mountains
According to the Meteorological Center, while the plains will continue to face rising heat, the mood of the mountains may change from Thursday. Starting April 16, districts like Pithoragarh, Uttarkashi, Rudraprayag, Chamoli, and Bageshwar are likely to experience moderate rain and thundershowers. This will bring relief to higher regions, though the plains will remain hot.
Impact on daily life
The rising heat has already begun troubling residents. In Dehradun, shopkeepers and office-goers are struggling with the harsh sun. Vehicle owners and laborers working outdoors are particularly affected. With temperatures climbing, health experts advise people to stay hydrated, avoid direct sunlight during peak hours, and wear light cotton clothing.
Tourism and trekking
The heatwave in the plains contrasts with snow-covered trekking routes in the higher Himalayas. While mountaineers may soon get access to Gangotri’s trails, casual tourists will need to wait until conditions improve. The expected rain from April 16 could help clear paths and make trekking safer.
Uttarakhand’s weather today reflects two extremes — scorching heat in the plains and snow in the mountains. With Dehradun recording 33°C and dry conditions persisting, residents brace for more heat. Relief is expected in the hills from April 16, but for now, the state remains under the grip of a worsening heatwave.
